user_prompt: Write for me `lib\ExtCodec\ExtCodec.cpp` and its associated header file. Please follow the guidelines `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md` as you write the code
There will be only one instance, make it a namespaced module, and it will work tightly with `AudioManager`
The codec is the `SGTL5000`, and I have added the files `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.cpp` and `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.h` to help this effort get started.

There will be a initialization function. This will first check the I2C address to see if it responds, and the rest of `AudioManager` and `RecordingView`behavior will depend on if it is available. This is to be done while the splash screen is still happening. Later we might even update the splash screen to indicate if an external CODEC is connected.
there should be a state enum like
GPIO35 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud is connected, a high-ish voltage means connected
GPIO36 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud has an inline mic, a low-ish voltage would indicate there’s no mic (as it will be shorted to HP-GND physically)

If the external codec is connected, then we will start up a dedicated ADC thread. It doesn’t need much stack. All it does is do ADC reads once every 100ms, blocking yet yielding. It sets the state of the module accordingly and maybe set an event flag. Other threads will use the state change to reconfigure electrically and reconfigure the GUI accordingly
Another mic is connected to line-in-right of the `SGTL5000` and it will be used if the earbud-inline-mic is not available
Remember that the `SGTL5000` will use stereo and a different BCLK pin for the I2S. The entire I2S does need to be reconfigured when we are switching between the SGTL5000 and the NS4168.
So in our current implementation, we are already operating under `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL`. No change in behavior needed

Under state `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD` we still have push-to-talk behavior, but instead of switching to the `SPM1423` when we push-to-talk, we switch to the `SGTL5000` using line-in-right as the sound source.

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using line-in-right, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using the `SGTL5000`'s dedicated mic input, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Add to `inc\pins.h`, the I2S_BCLK pin for the SGTL5000 will be GPIO13, the I2S_MCLK pin will be GPIO14 (and only used if the external codec is detected). GPIO34 is for the I2S data for the mic. GPIO2 is for the I2S data for the earbud. GPIO0 is for the I2S_LRCLK.
`AudioFifo` already has special enqueue and dequeue functions for handling stereo data, if these are missing then implement them
agent_reply: Created [ExtCodec.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:1) and [ExtCodec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:1) as a namespaced singleton-style module for SGTL5000 detection/state. It checks I2C address `0x0A`, initializes SGTL5000 control if present, starts a small ADC monitor task, tracks the four requested states, exposes state/event helpers, and has analog-path helpers for line-in-right vs dedicated mic.

Also added the SGTL5000/I2S/sense pins in [pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16), added tiny compatibility shims [AudioControl.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioControl.h:1) and [AudioStream.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioStream.h:1) so the starter SGTL5000 control files build, and call `ExtCodec::init()` during splash boot in [main.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:126).

I intentionally did not switch `AudioManager` into external-I2S routing yet, since its external I2S path is still stubbed and you asked current `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL` behavior to remain unchanged. `AudioFifo` already had stereo enqueue/dequeue functions, so I left that alone.

#9  0x400e3119 in lgfx::v1::i2c::readRegister(int, int, unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int, unsigned long) at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5GFX@0.2.20/src/lgfx/v1/platforms/esp32/../common.hpp:193
(inlined by) m5::I2C_Class::readRegister(unsigned char, unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int, unsigned long) const at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5Unified/src/utility/I2C_Class.cpp:77
#10 0x400e4d19 in m5::I2C_Device::readRegister(unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int) const at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5Unified/src/utility/imu/../I2C_Class.hpp:187
that's new, probably the codec checking and the battery tracker conflicting?
agent_reply: Yep, I think that was it. Not really codec ADC vs battery tracker, but the new codec code was using raw Arduino `Wire` on the same internal I2C bus that `M5.Power.getBatteryVoltage()` uses through M5Unified. The bare `Wire.begin()` in the SGTL5000 enable path was especially suspicious.
I patched it so the codec now shares M5Unified’s I2C path:
- [ExtCodec.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:258) probes with `M5.In_I2C.scanID()` and no longer calls `Wire.begin()`.
- Removed raw `Wire` use from the ExtCodec path.
One note: the builds regenerated `src/version.c`; `tools/test_builds/` is still untracked from before.

commit_message: fixed new i2c code from codec crashing
NS4168 works on stereo, added SGTL5000 config for our I2S format
